campaigns.title: Sanjivani- mother to 70 kids rescued from red-light areas, needs help to bring more to safety and care for them
campaigns.description: Renu grew up in the chawls of Pune’s red-light area in a dingy house, witnessing a flow of unknown men enter and leave their small bedroom day and night. Sometimes while looking at her mother hiding her fresh bruises with her pallu, Renu’s determination not to take up this path that her mother was pushed into only became stronger. She yearned for a way out of this dark world where she could live without fear, not struggle for three meals a day and get an education.Luckily for Renu (name changed), Swadhar IDWC-Pune took her in with her mother’s consent and put her in school. After years of hard work, she cleared all her grades and has now appeared for her Class 12 final exams.Swadhar IDWC led by Sanjivani Hingne, is on a mission to bring many more children to safety and your support can make it possible. Donate generously.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).A fight for the rights of childrenChildren like Renu, who are born to sex workers, cannot dream of a normal childhood. They are subjected to stress, trauma, and sometimes abuse by their mother’s clients. “What will the child get whose mother is a sex worker? There is no family; there is no schooling, no medical care, and nothing for the child. Children like them are easy prey. Our only concern is the child getting into mainstream society and not continuing in the mother’s profession,” says Sanjivani Hingne, secretary, Swadhar IDWC Pune.Swadhar IDWC- Pune weaves a safety net for children of sex workers. They strive to prevent second-generation prostitution of children living in red-light areas by providing a secure environment where they have access to shelter, nutrition, healthcare, education, and happiness. The organisation wants to provide a refuge for more children trapped in the sex-trade environment, but they cannot do it without your support.Project Mohor - a safe home away from red-light areasSwadhar’s fully functional 24x7 facility looks after the most vulnerable and neglected children of sex workers in Pune’s red-light area. These children often roam the streets till early morning while their mothers engage in prostitution. In view of providing a safe environment for them, Swadhar runs a kitchen, a pre-school, and an e-learning center.“Right from cleaning, bathing, feeding, medical care, seeing that the child is ready for school - all of it is taken care of here, which is impossible in a red-light area. So far, we have taken care of 200 children, most of them now pursuing higher education or have finished school with flying colours. Now, we need more funds because the number of children is increasing and we want to take in more children”.Unfortunately, this safe home for kids is at risk of shutting down. The NGO is racing against time to raise funds to build a new home for the rescued kids and your help can strengthen their efforts. Please donate as much as you can.Donate to this fundraiser on Give.do, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to help minor victims of forced prostitution get back to school and reshape their lives.

campaigns.title: Help 72-year-old Vanitha provide 100+ abandoned mentally ill women with shelter and care
campaigns.description: “The major problem in society today is the stigma against mentally ill or mentally disabled people. Families feed and care for men who are mentally ill and keep them at home. But no one wants to help women with mental illnesses,” says N Vanitha Rengaraj, chairperson of Sharanalayam.Sharanalaym’s ‘Jothi’ is a sanctuary for over 100 women facing mental challenges. Women with mental illness facing various challenges on the streets are brought to Sharanalayam with the help of the authorities and cared for. Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Donations to this fundraiser are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).The women at Sharanalaym Jothi are provided with necessities like food, clothing and medical care. They also get mental health services, access to psychiatrists and counseling, empowering individuals on their path to recovery.Once lost and vulnerable on unfamiliar streets, the women at Sharanalaym Jothi find solace.By supporting mentally disabled women at Sharanalaym Jothi, you contribute to creating a better world. We can ensure these women receive the care and support they deserve.Over the years, Vanitha has tried her best to run Sharanalayam from her own resources and that of her well-wishers, but the last few years have been harsh. “I normally don’t extend my hand for people to donate. Many have come on their own. But life has become a bit difficult now. I need your support to take care of the women here and also bring more women here in need of help,” says Vanitha. How Sharanalayam came into beingThe NGO started its operations in 2001 from rented premises in Pollachi in the district of Coimbatore. It started with taking care of children in difficult conditions. It slowly started taking care of mentally challenged women. Sharanalayam has come a long way since that time, with hundreds of children and women being taken care of by the NGO in the last two decades. Many women were rehabilitated by the NGO, thanks to its facilities.Sharanalayam needs urgent support to take care of over 100 women. The organisation also aspires to take care of many more mentally ill women in the coming months and years. Vanitha cannot do it without your support. Donate to this fundraiser on Give.do, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to help abandoned mentally ill women get care and shelter. Vanitha, 72, the founder of Sharanalayam cannot do it alone.Donate now.

campaigns.title: Help Leena in saving girls from being lost forever in red-light areas
campaigns.description: "Hundreds of young girls are lost forever in the dark lanes of red-light areas," says Leena Kejriwal, the founder of Missing Link Trust. These girls endure unimaginable abuse and exploitation, being lured or trafficked into an industry that breaks them physically and emotionally. Missing Link Trust is on a mission to prevent the tragedy before it happens, and also rehabilitate girls and women who are rescued from forced sex work.Leena’s journey began with her powerful street art project in Kolkata, where she painted haunting silhouettes of missing girls to raise awareness against sex trafficking. Now, she leads a larger fight — one that focuses on prevention and rehabilitation. But this mission needs your support.You can save countless girl children from going missing forever. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Preventing Trafficking Before It Begins“When girls come into a red-light area, the first thing is that they are broken, physically and emotionally,” Leena says. “It’s like they will be caught, hands and legs tied, and all heinous crimes committed on them.” These are the brutal realities that Missing Link Trust fights against every day.Many of these girls are from impoverished regions like the Sundarbans, and traffickers prey on their vulnerability. “It’s shocking that they sell themselves for as low as a hundred rupees,” Leena adds. "They ready her through violent acts, removing all threads of shame from her."But why wait until a girl is trafficked to save her? Missing Link Trust believes in stopping trafficking at its root — through “prevention, prevention, prevention” as Leena puts it. The goal is simple: protect girls from ever being lost to this darkness.A Safe World for Children“Our mission is the prevention of abuse and exploitation of children,” Leena emphasizes. “Our vision is to create a safe world for children, and we want to do this with a preventive methodology.”Every Donation MattersYour support will help Missing Link Trust continue its vital work of rescuing, rehabilitating, and preventing the trafficking of young girls. The funds raised will go directly towards:Prevention programs in vulnerable areas to stop trafficking before it happens.Rehabilitation services for girls rescued from red-light areas, providing them with medical care, emotional support, and education.Awareness campaigns to reach at-risk communities and ensure girls are protected from traffickers.Together, We Can Make a Difference“Why wait for a girl to get trafficked to save her?” asks Leena. With your help, we can stop this horror before it begins. Your donation will help us create a world where no girl is lost to the nightmare of trafficking.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest and most trusted online giving platform, to support Missing Link Trust to save young girls from being trafficked and help rehabilitate those rescued.

campaigns.title: Help visually impaired ‘Singh Sir’ provide food, shelter and education to blind girls
campaigns.description: In many families, blind girls are often seen as a burden—a harsh reality that leaves many of them abandoned, neglected, and deprived of opportunities. Families often feel, "If we spend money on them, it would be useless", shares Ramesh Singh a blind teacher committed to providing care and education the blind girls deserve. Sadly, many blind girls are forced to grow up in isolation and denied the education and care they deserve. But amidst this darkness, there is a glimmer of hope. “Singh Sir” as he is fondly called, is on a mission but he cannot do it alone. Support this blind teacher in transforming lives. Donate generouslyDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).How it beganIn 2005, Ramesh Prasad Singh, a blind teacher who has personally experienced the struggles faced by blind children, founded the Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad. His mission? To rescue blind girls from neglect and give them a chance to thrive."People think this girl is a lifelong burden for us," Ramesh says. "But as soon as the girl gets a job, the same family starts hounding her."Over the years, the nonprofit has provided food, shelter, and admission fees for college to blind girls, ensuring they are not trapped within four walls but instead empowered to pursue their dreams.At the heart of Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad is a home—a safe haven for blind girls where they receive education, care, and the tools to build a better future. These girls are not just surviving; they are thriving. They are becoming independent, proving that blindness is not a limitation but a stepping stone to resilience and strength.But Ramesh cannot continue this mission alone.He appeals to you to join hands and support Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad.Why your contribution countsNutritious meals for blind girlsSafe and secure shelterAdmission fees and educational support"We appeal to each person to support our mission and make a difference," Ramesh urges.Together, we can ensure these girls are no longer seen as burdens but as empowered individuals ready to lead fulfilling lives.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest online donation platform, to support Bhartiya Netraheen Kalyan Parishad in its mission to provide blind girls the shelter, care and education they deserve.

campaigns.title: Help Raja Bhaiyya care for farmer suicide families and prevent others from ending their lives
campaigns.description: “There was a farmer called Bihor. He had a debt of ₹6 lakh. His mother fell ill and had to spend a lot of money on her care. His land was not giving any yield and his crop was destroyed. Pressure from the banks and the money lenders because of debt left him with no other option but to commit suicide,” shares Raja Bhaiyya, the founder of Vidya Dham Samiti, an NGO that helps families in which farmers have committed suicide and prevents further farmer suicides from happening. “His family was in tatters, but we helped them. We gave them foodgrains and we gave them training in sustainable farming. After all these efforts, the family is in decent condition.” This Makar Sankranti, you can help Raja Bhaiyya support farmer families and prevent more farmer suicides from taking place by equipping them with rations and tools to have better futures. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Vidya Dham Samiti identifies farmer’s households where farmer suicides have taken place and farmer households that are prone to suicide. For these families, the NGO offers counselling, skill development training and capacity building to train the farmers and their families in alternate agricultural practices so they can continue their livelihoods, access available Government schemes and cope with conditions such as drought and climate change through new and other agricultural technologies and practices - and not take the drastic step of suicide. Founded in 2001 by Raja Bhaiyya, Vidya Dham Samiti focuses its work in and around Bundelkhand, Banda District in Uttar Pradesh which is an area that is prone to drought and crop failure. Your support can prevent farmer suicides and empower farmer families with counselling and skills training. Donate now. “The farmers who are cut off from the mainstream and are in trouble,” explains Vidya Dham Samiti founder Raja Bhaiyya. “We bring them together and counsel them, we give them exposure and provide them with every opportunity to do well through fertilizer, grains and agricultural implements. We train them to adopt the right methods to do agriculture and we give them top-quality grains. These are our efforts.”“But these efforts are taking place at a small level. We want to take our programme to a larger level. We can save the lives of farmers if we can bring more and more people into our programmes and reach more and more areas.” Donate now to help Raja Bhaiyya support farmer families that have been devastated by suicide and prevent other farmer suicides from happening. “We can stop farmer suicides through training, counselling and newer forms of agriculture. For those farmers who are on the verge of suicide, and whose families are in trouble, please give them one more chance. Donate so that we can rescue them from suicide.”Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest online donation platform, to help Raja Bhaiya rescue debt-trapped farmers who are on the verge of suicide.

campaigns.title: Help feed devotees during Puri Jagannath Rath Yatra from  July 16-24
campaigns.description: Every year, the sacred Jagannath Rath Yatra draws millions to witness Lord Jagannath's chariot procession. Among them are thousands of elderly and other devotees who travel from remote villages, carrying little beyond their faith. What they don't carry is food.The Yatra stretches across hours, often through heavy monsoon rain. Pilgrims stand for long periods, pressed into crowds, waiting for a glimpse of the deities. Many have no money left for a meal after the cost of travel.Pure Devotion Foundation (PDF) stands in this gap.Through its Yatra meal programme, the PDF serves warm, simple meals to devotees at points along the pilgrimage route, food handed over with the same reverence the devotees bring to their prayers. For many of these devotees, this meal is the only one they will eat that day. The need grows every year, as the crowds do. Volunteers on the ground report the same scene repeatedly: elderly men and women, drenched from the rain, sitting on the roadside because they can no longer stand — some too proud to ask for help, some too weak to. PDF prepares meals in bulk, transports to the route, and serves fresh through the duration of the Yatra, regardless of rain, crowd density, or logistical hurdles.You can be the reason a devotee doesn't go hungry this Yatra season.A contribution, however modest, helps Pure Devotion Foundation prepare and serve one more meal, reach one more devotee.Give.do is India's largest and most trusted online donation platform, connecting donors directly to verified NGOs like Pure Devotion Foundation.This Rath Yatra, help ensure that faith isn't met with hunger. Donate today, and let no devotee go without a meal on their sacred journey.

campaigns.title: Help abandoned elderly with shelter and care
campaigns.description: Every day, elderly parents are left alone on the streets, at train stations, or even outside temples. Many are sick, struggling with age-related problems, and have no money or family to turn to. Some suffer for weeks or months before anyone notices their pain. The heartbreaking truth? They were abandoned by their own families.Although Indian law requires children and relatives to care for senior citizens, many elders still face neglect, abuse, and abandonment. The problem is only getting worse.But there is hope. Our dedicated NGO partners work day and night to rescue these forgotten elders. They provide safe shelter, medical care, nutritious meals, and most importantly—love and respect. Through old age homes across the country, our partners give these seniors a chance to live their remaining years with dignity, comfort, and peace.You can make a difference. Your donation will help our NGO partners:Rescue homeless elders from the streetsProvide shelter, food, and medical careRestore their faith in humanity with kindness and supportNo elder should spend their final years alone and suffering. Your generosity can give them a fresh start. Donate today and help an abandoned senior citizen find safety, care, and hope.

campaigns.title: Help Disabled Children with Shelter & Care
campaigns.description: I am fundraising for a cause close to my heart, to bring a lasting change in the lives of the underprivileged. To help me create more impact and touch more lives, I need your support and contribution. Please back my fundraiser so that together we can build a better India for all of us. Pledge your support, donate now!

campaigns.title: RESCUE AND CARE OF DESTITUTE CHILDREN
campaigns.description:
More than 12 million destitute children in India. These children are found abandoned by the police in toilets, railway stations, religious places, hospitals and dustbins. These children are often left to mercy of stray animals, insects, anti-social elements and unhygienic conditions.   The children are rescued by Police, Citizens, NGOs and are in immediate care and protection.  There are also children who are surrendered by their birth parents due to social and medical reasons.  The children need shelter, food, medical, education, development, counseling and rehabilitation.   
 Bal Asha Trust gives children love, care, medical attention from the best pediatricians and the required treatment is provided in top hospitals. We have committed team of trained professionals including pediatricians, social workers, para medical, speech & occupational therapist, psychologists, teachers and care takers. We care for healthy children with many others with special needs, physical or mental challenges and those who suffer from conditions like heart issues, CP, Hep. B & HIV etc 
 We believe that every child should grow up in a family and make concerted efforts to reunite runaway missing children with their families across India. Children are placed with loving and caring Adoptive Families through a legal process. The Trust has been successful in placing many physically mentally and medically special needs children in Adoption. Every child deserves quality life while in a home and a chance to socially get integrated in a family by finding a loving forever family.  The trust will also create awareness on the issue.

campaigns.title: Provide Diya Foundation the space to nurture and train people with intellectual disabilities
campaigns.description:
The road to the Diya foundation is inaccessible and terrible for people with wheelchairs, and the main mud road is uneven and difficult to walk on. The training rooms in the existing building have to be fabricated, and the spaces are small, with insufficient space for people to move around. There is little greenery outside the building and no open space for people to sit outside and enjoy the serenity of nature. We are looking at expanding as there are new parents interested in enrolling in the training program, due to insufficiency we need to accommodate only a few young adults. We’ve been paying a peak rent of 1 lakh monthly, which has become unsustainable. Our land/ premises will give us a sense of belonging and permanence in the community. 
 We have raised one crore towards our goal, but we need three crores to acquire land and construct the building. To achieve our goal we are appealing to Bangalore-based companies and individual volunteers to run for Diya at the TCS World 10k Run 2023. We are also connecting with our past supporters, volunteers, and interns. This year marks the 25th anniversary of Diya for the silver jubilee we would like to showcase quarterly our varied training programs, also display our coffee table book, share engaging stories of change, and celebrate our trainee's journey. 
 The aim is to improve the training program, focusing on designing spacious training rooms that are conducive to learning, and skilling. Getting our premises can help accommodate young adults and help interested parents work together. The vision is to impact 500 more students with intellectual disabilities with vocational training and support them in finding employment. Our vision is also to create a pool of 250 employable adults who are ready to meet the job market as they get skilled. Together we can celebrate their path of change.
